blocks.content: The DJ said something into the microphone and the tempo dropped.

Around them the crowd shifted — some people stepping off the floor, others pulling together, the whole room rearranging into pairs. Savannah leaned into Marcus. Eli, characteristically, materialized beside a girl from the junior class and said something that made her laugh.
He looked at her. She looked at him. The song was starting — something slow, the kind of song that gave you an excuse if you needed one.

He offered his hand. She took it. They moved to an open space on the floor, not the center — somewhere between the lights and the edge — and he put his hand at her waist and she put hers on his shoulder, and they started to move in the easy way of two people who have been in sync since they were twelve years old.
It was fine. It was completely, totally fine.
